tasks.title: Add a dedicated lead-sheet-team supervisor and state directory
acceptance_criteria: The repository includes a separate supervisor runtime under scripts/lead-sheet-team.
Lead-sheet-team stores run state under a dedicated directory instead of ./.ai/runs.
The new runtime can initialize, route, validate, and print work packages without depending on the existing development team supervisor.
done_definition: Lead-sheet-team commands exist in package.json and basic lifecycle commands execute successfully.

tasks.title: Define role prompts and state schemas for the extraction team
acceptance_criteria: The repository includes dedicated agent prompts for intake, preprocessing, extraction, alignment, verification, review, and publishing.
Lead-sheet-team has dedicated schema contracts for its run and artifact files.
The new contracts emphasize extraction accuracy and explicit uncertainty handling.
done_definition: A fresh operator can inspect the prompts and schemas and understand the new team's workflow.

assumptions: This first pass creates orchestration scaffolding only, not extraction logic.
Lead-sheet-team should remain operationally independent even if it later writes to shared database models.
The existing development AI team remains the mechanism for implementing code inside this repository.
risks: If the lead-sheet-team schemas are too narrow, later extraction work may require contract changes.
If team boundaries are not documented clearly, users may confuse lead-sheet-team with the existing development supervisor.
